title: What to say when a client ghosts you
dek: Silence after a great tour is not rejection. Treat it like rejection and it becomes one.
heroAlt: What to say when a client ghosts you

blocks.html: The tour went well. You know it did. They lingered in the kitchen, asked about move-in dates, said they would be in touch. That was nine days ago. Your last two texts sit on read, and the story in your head is getting darker by the hour.

Here is the boring truth about ghosting: it is almost never about you. Approvals stall, jobs wobble, partners disagree, timelines slide. The client is not avoiding you. They are avoiding an unresolved decision, and your message is a reminder of it. Which means the fix is not persistence. It is lowering the cost of replying.

blocks.text: Ghosts do not need pressure. They need an easy way back into the conversation.

items.title: The no-guilt check-in.
items.html: Hey, totally understand if the timeline moved. Want me to keep an eye out for you or pause for now? Two easy outs, both of which restart the dialogue.

items.title: The new-information drop.
items.html: That unit you liked just dropped $150. No response needed, just keeping you posted. Value with zero ask.

items.title: The clean close.
items.html: Going to assume the search is on hold. If anything changes I am one text away. Certainty beats limbo, and this message gets more replies than any pitch.

blocks.html: Send the no-guilt check-in to every ghost from the last thirty days, today. Expect a third of them to answer. Ghosts are usually just people who did not know how to come back.
The Playbook carries the full script vault for every stage of the client lifecycle, including the awkward ones nobody teaches.

blocks.lead: Every re-engagement script, ready to send.
